description:
You are maintaining a tiny sample repository for a fictional Kubernetes operator that lives in

/home/user/k8s-operator

The repository already contains two important text files:

1. /home/user/k8s-operator/VERSION  
   • single-line file that **currently** contains only  
     1.2.3

2. /home/user/k8s-operator/CHANGELOG.md  
   • markdown file whose first lines **currently** look exactly like this (note the trailing blank line after the section):

```
## [1.2.3] - 2023-07-01
### Changed
- Fixed reconciliation loop bug.

```

Task
----

Perform a semantic *minor* version bump from 1.2.3 → 1.3.0 and create a matching changelog entry.

When you are done, the files must look **exactly** like the following:

A) /home/user/k8s-operator/VERSION  
   (one line, no trailing spaces)
```
1.3.0
```

B) /home/user/k8s-operator/CHANGELOG.md  
   (the new section must be *prepended* on top of the existing content, followed by a single blank line before the old section begins)

```
## [1.3.0] - 2023-08-01
### Added
- Promote CRD manifests to v1.

### Changed
- Updated deployment manifests to use image tag v1.3.0.

## [1.2.3] - 2023-07-01
### Changed
- Fixed reconciliation loop bug.

```

After updating the files, display the first 12 lines of /home/user/k8s-operator/CHANGELOG.md so the result can be automatically checked.

Constraints & hints
-------------------
• The task should be achievable with **one** reasonably short shell command, but use whatever approach you prefer.  
• Do **not** remove or alter the pre-existing 1.2.3 section. Only prepend the new 1.3.0 section exactly as shown above.  
• No root privileges are available; work inside /home/user only.  
• Remember to keep a blank line between the 1.3.0 section and the old 1.2.3 section, and no extra blank line at the very top of the file.

Verification
------------
The automated checker will validate:

1. /home/user/k8s-operator/VERSION contains exactly “1.3.0”.
2. /home/user/k8s-operator/CHANGELOG.md starts with the 9 lines shown above, in the precise order, spacing, and punctuation.
3. Printing the first 12 lines of CHANGELOG.md must reveal those 9 new lines followed immediately by the old “## [1.2.3] …” header.
